paper_title: Cytogenetic study and pollen viability of Diatenopteryx sorbifolia Radlk.

methods.method_name: Meiotic Analysis Sample Preparation and Observation
methods.description: Procedure for collecting, fixing, staining, and analyzing floral buds to study meiotic behavior.

steps.description: During the flowering phenophase, pre-anthesis floral buds were collected, between October 2013 and November 2015.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect plant samples (floral buds) during specific phenophase and time period

steps.description: fixed in Carnoy (ethanol-acetic acid 3:1)
steps.searchable_summary: Fix biological samples using Carnoy solution

steps.description: remaining for 24 hours at room temperature
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate fixed samples at room temperature for a specified duration

steps.description: then transferred to a 70% alcohol solution
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer fixed samples to alcohol storage solution

steps.description: and finally kept under refrigeration (6 to 10°C) until analysis
steps.searchable_summary: Store biological samples under refrigeration until needed for analysis

steps.description: When preparing slides, the anthers were crushed and the myocytes stained with 1% of propionic carmine, according to Guerra and Souza (2002)
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare microscopy slides from plant tissue by crushing and staining with propionic carmine

steps.description: For the meiotic behavior analysis were analyzed from metaphase phase I until microspores tetrad
steps.searchable_summary: Observe and analyze meiotic stages (Metaphase I to Tetrad) under microscope
outputs: Observations of meiotic stages and behavior

steps.description: counting at least 500 microsporocytes per accession
steps.searchable_summary: Count a minimum number of specific cell types (microsporocytes) per sample (accession)

methods.method_name: Mitotic Analysis Sample Preparation and Observation
methods.description: Procedure for collecting, germinating, pretreating, fixing, staining, and analyzing roots to study mitotic behavior.

steps.description: During the fruiting period, ripe fruits were collected for the later seed processing
steps.searchable_summary: Collect plant samples (ripe fruits/seeds) during specific phenophase

steps.description: and then germinated in acrylic boxes with lid, containing vermiculite of sterilized, moistened medium particle size
steps.searchable_summary: Germinate plant seeds in a controlled environment using specific substrate
inputs: Sterilized, moistened medium particle size vermiculite

steps.description: When the primary roots had about 2 cm, they were collected
steps.searchable_summary: Collect plant tissue (primary roots) when they reach a specific length

steps.description: and pretreated with 8-hydroxyquinoline solution (8HQ) at 0.002M for 24h at 6° to 10°C, to obtain a higher number of cells in metaphase
steps.searchable_summary: Pretreat plant roots with 8-hydroxyquinoline solution under specific conditions to accumulate metaphase cells

steps.description: Finally, the roots were washed
steps.searchable_summary: Wash biological samples after pretreatment

steps.description: and fixed in Carnoy (ethanol-acetic acid 3: 1)
steps.searchable_summary: Fix biological samples using Carnoy solution

steps.description: remaining for 24 hours at room temperature
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate fixed samples at room temperature for a specified duration

steps.description: and then stored in alcohol 70% (6 a 10°C)
steps.searchable_summary: Store biological samples in alcohol solution under refrigeration

steps.description: Schiff staining technique was used for the preparation of the slides.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare microscopy slides using the Schiff staining technique

steps.description: The roots were washed in distilled water
steps.searchable_summary: Wash biological samples in distilled water (part of staining protocol)

steps.description: hydrolyzed in HCl 1N at 60°C for 10 minutes
steps.searchable_summary: Hydrolyze biological samples with acid and heat (part of staining protocol)

steps.description: and then washed again
steps.searchable_summary: Wash biological samples after hydrolysis (part of staining protocol)

steps.description: and placed in the dye, remaining immersed for 30 minutes
steps.searchable_summary: Stain biological samples by immersing in dye (Schiff reagent) for a specified duration

steps.description: The root end was crushed with a drop of 1% propionic carmine
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare root tip squash slide by crushing tissue and adding stain

steps.description: Five to ten metaphases per genotype were counted
steps.searchable_summary: Count a specific number of metaphase cells per sample (genotype)

steps.description: and the metaphases that presented well-condensed, visible, spread and non-overlapping chromosomes on the slide were considered for characterization and photographic registration
steps.searchable_summary: Select metaphase cells based on specific morphological criteria for detailed characterization and imaging
inputs: Morphological criteria (chromosome condensation, visibility, spread, non-overlapping)

steps.description: The germination of the seeds was done for all the genotypes. However, only the G5 was possible to make the analyzes. The remaining phase M cells were not observed.
steps.searchable_summary: Attempt seed germination for all genotypes and note limitations in subsequent analysis

methods.description: Procedure for viewing prepared slides (meiotic and mitotic) using a microscope and capturing images. Includes criteria for counting different cell types.

steps.description: Both meiotic and mitotic analyzes were performed on an Olympus X31TM image capture microscope
steps.searchable_summary: Perform microscopic analysis using a specific model of image capture microscope

steps.description: where the most representative phases were captured with the aid of the Pixelview station v5 23 TV software
steps.searchable_summary: Capture digital images of representative microscopic fields using specific software

steps.description: and scanned by Corel Photo-Paint X6
steps.searchable_summary: Process or edit captured microscopy images using image editing software

steps.description: Cells counted: empty cells, where no genetic material was found; cells with incrustations, which crystals prevented the genetic material visualization and the cells with abnormalities, which were considered when any abnormality was observed in the segregation of the chromosomes.
steps.searchable_summary: Count and categorize cells based on visual criteria (empty, incrustations, abnormalities) from microscopy images or observation
outputs: Counts of different cell categories (empty, incrustations, abnormalities)
